HDFC Small Cap Fund Direct Growth - NAV | HDFC Mutual Fund
HDFC Small Cap FundEquityDIRECTREGULARAn open ended equity scheme predominantly investing in small cap stocksToday’s Beginners Could Be Tomorrow’s LeadersWhat is HDFC Small Cap Fund?HDFC Small Cap Fund is an open-ended equity mutual fund scheme that predominantly invests in small-cap companies. Small-cap companies typically represent emerging businesses with strong growth potential.By investing in a diversified portfolio of small-cap stocks, the fund aims to identify companies with scalable business models and participate in their long-term growth journey.Investment Objective is to provide long-term capital appreciation/ income by investing predominantly in small-cap companies. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the scheme will be achieved.Investment Strategy of HDFC Small Cap FundHDFC Small Cap Fund focuses on identifying high-quality small-cap companies with long-term growth potential trading at reasonable valuations.The portfolio construction emphasizes:Bottom-up stock selection based on company fundamentalsFocus on quality companies, with sound financial strength and reasonable return on equityDiversification across sectors and industriesInvest at sensible valuations in companies trading at reasonable multiplesWhy invest in HDFC Small Cap Fund?Untapped Universe: With more than 1000 companies beyond the top 250 by market cap, the small-cap space offers a wider scope for alpha generation.Under-Researched = Opportunity: Many small-cap stocks are less tracked by analysts, allowing fund managers to identify mispriced winners early.Potential Re-rating: As small companies grow, they attract institutional interest and better coverage, often leading to higher valuations.Proven Strategy: The fund has a 17+ year track record of spotting scalable, quality small-cap businesses with sound management.Smart Diversification: Adds potential for high-growth to your portfolio, complementing large and mid-cap exposure of investorsWho can consider HDFC Small Cap Fund?This fund may suit investors who:Seek long-term capital appreciation (5+ years)Are comfortable with short-term volatilityWant exposure to emerging growth businessesAim to diversify across market-cap segmentsHow to start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund?Investors can start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund through the following steps:Complete your KYCKeep PAN, Aadhaar and bank details ready.Visit HDFC Mutual Fund Website / AppGo to the fund page and click Invest Now.Choose Investment ModeSIP – invest a fixed amount monthlyLump Sum – invest one timeSelect Plan & OptionDirect or Regular PlanGrowth or IDCW optionEnter Amount & Payment DetailsSet SIP date or make payment online.Confirm & Start InvestingYour investment will be processed and units will be allotted.Returnssince inception18.36%Inception Date01/01/2013RiskometerVery HighMin SIP₹ 100Ideal forWealth Creation3 Years and aboveEntry LoadAn entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads.
NATERTERThe Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses.DisclaimerIncluding Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio0.73Lock inA lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in.
NANAV NANet Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date.
NAAUM (30/04/2026)Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors.
₹38,168.18 Cr.BenchmarkA benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market.

HDFC Small Cap Fund dated November 21, 2025KIM - HDFC Small Cap Fund dated November 21, 2025Note - Why Invest in HDFC Small Cap Fund - Nov 2025 HDFC Small Cap Fund - Presentation (October 2025)Fund Facts - HDFC Small Cap Fund - April 26Exit LoadIn respect of each purchase / switch-in of Units, an Exit Load of 1.00% is payable if Units are redeemed / switched out within 1 year from the date of allotment.No Exit Load is payable if Units are redeemed / switched out after 1 year from the date of allotment.Product LabellingBenchmark RiskometerThis product is suitable for investors who are seeking~to generate long-term capital appreciation/incomeinvestments predominantly in Small-Cap companies ~Investors should consult their financial advisers if in doubt about whether the product is suitable for them. FAQsWhat is a small-cap fund?A small-cap fund invests mainly in companies ranked beyond the top 250 by market capitalization.Is this fund risky?Small-cap funds tend to be more volatile but may offer higher growth potential over the long term.What is the ideal investment horizon?Typically 5 years or more.Can beginners invest?Yes, if they understand equity market volatility and invest for long-term goals.SIP or lump sum: which is better?SIP helps average costs and reduce timing risk.Does the fund only invest in small caps?Primarily yes, but it can allocate a portion to large and mid caps for flexibility.Is this suitable for retirement planning?It can form part of a diversified long-term retirement portfolio but is typically not a standalone option.What role does this fund play in a portfolio?It aims to enhance long-term growth potential within a diversified equity portfolio.What benchmark does HDFC Small Cap Fund track?HDFC Small Cap Fund is benchmarked against BSE 250 SmallCap Index (TRI)What is the minimum investment in HDFC Small Cap Fund?Rs.100/- and any amount thereafterHow is HDFC Small Cap Fund different from a large-cap fund?A small-cap fund invests primarily in smaller companies, whereas large-cap funds invest mainly in well-established companies with larger market capitalisation. Small-cap funds may offer higher growth potential but can also experience higher volatility.Ask Me
